content.full_text:
TELEPHONE ADVICE

DATE: 4/16/91
FILE # M-91-245
REQUESTER: City of Los Angeles (213) 485-4792
CONSULTANT: Cassady, L

QUESTION

May a candidate file a separate Form 501 (Candidate of Intention) and a Form 502 (Bank Account) for a city primary and city run-off election?

If yes, must a candidate establish separate committees for the primary and the run-off election?

CONCLUSION

A candidate may designate a primary and a run-off election as separate elections.

FACT:

The City of Los Angeles' local campaign ordinance requires candidates to establish separate bank accounts for the primary and the run-off election.

ADVICE:

The Act does not require that an "election" encompass both a primary and a run-off vote. Therefore, candidates in the jurisdiction of the city of Los Angeles may designate the primary as a separate and distinct election from the run-off election. In such situations a candidate can indicate on a Form 501 (Candidate Intention) and a Form 502 (Campaign Bank Account) the primary election date as the election date. Similarly, candidates in the run-off election may file new Forms 501 and 502 and designate the date of the election as the run-off election date.

Under the circumstances described above, a candidate must establish separate committees for the primary and the run-off election. (Regulation 18521.)

REGULATIONS: 18521
ACT: 84101, 85200, 85201
OPINIONS:
ADVICE LETTERS:
OTHER:
